Suppose we want to create a linear regression model that predicts a penguin's bill depth y using both their Thpper length x1 and body
mass #2, plus an intercept term.
y = 00 + 01x1 + +2x2
Another way to write this:
bill_depth_mm = 00 +01 * flipper_length_mm + 02 * body_mass_g
OLS Approach 1: Use Solution to Normal Equation
We saw in last lecture that we can model the above multiple linear regression equation using matrix multiplication:
Y = X0
The optimal 0 that minimizes MSE also solves the normal equation:
(XTV)Â-XTV
In order to fit a column, or excuse me, in order to fit a model with an
intercept, we need a column of ones.
If X is full column rank, then there is a unique solution to v.
